response.justification: The candidate brings solid full-stack engineering depth and Staff-level tenure across multiple organizations, with recent exposure to MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq. However, the role requires 8+ years of production AI delivery — the candidate's AI work is limited to roughly one month at the current employer. Python, the primary language for applied AI engineering, is entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI), and customer-facing technical leadership is not demonstrated.
strengths: Staff-level titles at multiple companies confirm seniority and breadth across distributed systems, DevOps, and full-stack delivery
Direct hands-on experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure — a key technology explicitly listed in the JD
High-scale distributed systems background (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby) demonstrates performance engineering fluency relevant to production AI workloads
concerns: Python is completely absent from the skills list and every role's tech stack — a critical gap given it is the primary language requirement for this position
No mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM performance metrics, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI
AI/GenAI work is limited to approximately one month at the current employer; the role mandates 8+ years building and shipping production-grade AI solutions

key_factors.factor: No Python and missing core AI stack (RAG, vector DBs, LLMs)
Insufficient AI production experience tenure (months vs. 8+ years required)
MCP and agentic workflow infrastructure experience at current role
